calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER FRANCES SMITH'S Motion To Compel Arbitration

Granted. No opposition filed.

Counsel for the petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSCECUTE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
John Henning for Petitioner.
Sammy Daboussi (949-221-1069; sdaboussi@bremerwhyte.com) of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara, LLP for Respondent.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Respondent James River Insurance Company's motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is denied.

The petition to compel arbitration was filed October 15, 2018. Respondent filed this motion on December 17, 2020. The court may not dismiss an action for delay in prosecution unless (1) service is not made within two years after the action is commenced against the defendant; (2) the action is not brought to trial within three years, or within two years under certain circumstances; or (3) a new trial is granted. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 583.420(a).)

"The times provided in subdivision (a) shall be computed in the manner provided for computation of the comparable times under . . . Section 583.310." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 583.420(b).) The Judicial Council's Emergency Rules Related to Covid-19 extended the time in which to bring an action to trial under Section 583.310 by an additional six months. (Rule 10(a).) Moreover, for the period between March 18, 2020, and June 1, 2020, the Court's general orders suspending court operations made proceeding with a hearing on the petition to compel arbitration "impossible, impracticable, or futile." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 583.340(c).) Thus, the discretionary two-year period arguably has not yet expired, and none of the statutory grounds for dismissal under Section 583.420(a) is met here. However, the Court cautions claimant that it must proceed diligently to notice its motion to compel for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To Dimiss For Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To Ccp 583.410(A)
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on RESPONDENT JAMES RIVER INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Dimiss For Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To CCP 583.410(A) is adopted as follows:

Respondent's motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is denied. The petition to compel arbitration was filed October 15, 2018. Respondent filed this motion on December 26, 2019. The court may not dismiss an action for delay in prosecution unless (1) service is not made within two years after the action is commenced against the defendant; (2) the action is not brought to trial within three years, or within two years under certain circumstances; or (3) a new trial is granted. (Code Civ. Proc. 583.420(a).) None of the statutory grounds is met here.

Claimant's purported "understanding that the Court would eventually grant or deny Claimant's petition to compel arbitration" (Opp. at 2) merely because it filed a petition to compel arbitration is mistaken. Claimant must notice the petition for hearing, file and serve a supporting memorandum and declarations, etc. (See generally California Practice Guide: Alternative Dispute Resolution (The Rutter Group 2019) ch. 5-G.)

Counsel for Claimant is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
